homepage.text_excerpt: Claire Fox Skip to content Claire Fox Menu Welcome About Claire Media Archive Inside the Lords Events Publications Reclaiming Democracy I STILL Find That Offensive! Welcome This website is a home for my articles, broadcast interviews, my work as the Director of the Academy of Ideas and an archive of my contribution as a Brexit Party MEP. It will also be the space in which I will publish my thoughts, ideas and contributions as a new peer in the House of Lords. In the upper house, I will be known as Baroness Fox of Buckley and despite the obvious irony, I am keen to use my time there to further the causes of democratic accountability and free speech. So I will be speaking freely about my experiences on this website. But this week’s judgment marks a major step backwards for free speech on campus. pages.text_excerpt: About Claire – Claire Fox Skip to content Claire Fox Menu Welcome About Claire Media Archive Inside the Lords Events Publications Reclaiming Democracy I STILL Find That Offensive! About Claire Claire is a writer, broadcaster, free-speech campaigner and author of I Find That Offensive! She is a panellist on Radio 4’s The Moral Maze and appears in UK media frequently, including on Newsnight , Any Questions and Sky Newspaper Review . Claire is a member of the European Cultural Parliament and director and founder of the Academy of Ideas . Claire Fox was elected Brexit Party MEP for North West England at the 2019 European Parliament election and served until the UK left the EU on 31 January 2020. The Academy was founded in 2000 to provide a forum committed to open and robust public debate in which ideas can be interrogated, argued for and fought over. The Academy organises intelligent public debates, on controversial topics, and most importantly challenges contemporary knee-jerk orthodoxies. One focus of the work is to ensure that new generations look behind the headlines and avoid over simplistic conclusions on difficult topics. To this end, The Academy organises a range of high-profile public events, including a yearly two-day festival, the Battle of Ideas . The Academy also founded the acclaimed sixth-form debating competition Debating Matters and Living Freedom, a residential school in London for 18- to 25- year-olds. In the European Parliament, Claire was a member of the Culture and Education committee and the Industry, Research and Energy committee. She stood as an MEP to represent those people in the North West who voted out of the European Union and, whilst recognising that the European Parliament is a relatively powerless institution, she aimed to represent her constituents’ interests in the EP as well as regionally in the North West. In August 2020, Claire was nominated to become a Peer in the House of Lords, and was introduced into the House as Baroness Fox of Buckley on the 8th of October. In the Lords, Claire continues to raise awareness of issues around free speech, censorship and the promotion of civil debate. ‘I STILL Find That Offensive!’ – Book available now Search for: Claire Fox on Twitter Retweet on Twitter Claire Fox Retweeted The Free Speech Union @speechunion · 2 May As @lara_e_brown writes in @spectator, for a time it seemed we finally had a regulator able to uphold free speech in higher education. pages.text_excerpt: Media – Claire Fox Skip to content Claire Fox Menu Welcome About Claire Media Archive Inside the Lords Events Publications Reclaiming Democracy I STILL Find That Offensive! Media Rod Liddle, 11th April 2026 Thursday 16 April 2026 Saturday 11 April 2026 by Harley Richardson On Times Radio, Claire joined academic Lisa McKenzie and presenter Rod Liddle to review the stories of the week. They discussed the prospect of a peace deal in the Iran war, the legacy of the Artemis moon mission, the Premier League With Pride campaign, fuel crisis protests in Ireland, changes to eligibility for council housing, … Read more Categories Media Tags Rod Liddle , Times Radio Women’s rights one year on from the Supreme Court ruling Thursday 16 April 2026 Sunday 5 April 2026 by Harley Richardson Claire spoke to podcaster Penny Dee about the Supreme Court ruling on sex and single sex spaces – the gap between legal clarity and real world implementation, and why so many institutions still appear reluctant to act. Categories Media Tags Penny Dee Lords vote to scrap non-crime hate incidents Saturday 14 March 2026 Thursday 12 March 2026 by Harley Richardson Claire spoke to TalkTV‘s Kevin O’Sullivan about the defeat of an attempt to continue the recording of non-crime hate incidents. Categories Media Tags Kevin O'Sullivan , talkTV Free speech on life support Tuesday 17 March 2026 Thursday 12 March 2026 by Harley Richardson In conversation with Giuseppe Gracia of Schweizer Monat, the Swiss ‘magazine for doers and thinkers’, Claire discussed the fragile state of free speech in the UK, pressured by expanding hate and harm laws and a culture that treats offence as injury. Categories Media Tags Giuseppe Gracia , Schweizer Monat Claire on the DM-MI podcast Saturday 28 February 2026 Thursday 19 February 2026 by Harley Richardson Claire appeared on the DM-MI podcast, where you can find conversations around Technology & Business, as well as passionate discussions on a range of topics from Mixed Martial Arts to History, Geo-Politics and Current Affairs. Speaking with host Sukh Hayre, Claire shared her unconventional political journey from revolutionary left activism to the House of Lords, … Read more Categories Media Tags DM-MI , Sukh Hayre TalkTV, 17th February 2026 Wednesday 18 February 2026 Tuesday 17 February 2026 by Harley Richardson Claire spoke to Julia Hartley-Brewer about compensation for ‘wrongful’ phone seizures by border agents, the Filipino migrant who was given sex change treatments on the NHS, the offer of ‘US refuge’ for the British man who burnt a Koran, and revelations about Labour’s reaction to a corruption dossier. Categories Media Tags Julia Hartley-Brewer , talkTV The Cabal Play Thursday 19 February 2026 Tuesday 10 February 2026 by Harley Richardson On The Mike Graham Show, Baroness Claire Fox delivered a blistering assessment of Keir Starmer’s leadership crisis, Labour’s internal chaos, and a political class increasingly detached from the voters it claims to represent. Categories Media Tags Mike Graham Starmer and Mandelson Sunday 8 February 2026 Friday 6 February 2026 by Harley Richardson “I almost don’t want him to resign over this… there’s so many reasons why he’s not fit to govern…” Claire spoke to TalkTV‘s Julia Hartley-Brewer about Keir Starmer’s handling of the Peter Mandelson scandal. Categories Media Tags Julia Hartley-Brewer , talkTV Newsnight, 29th January 2026 Monday 2 February 2026 Thursday 29 January 2026 by Harley Richardson Claire was a guest on Newsnight. Events United we stand? Ukraine and the future of the West Tuesday 10 May 2022 Wednesday 20 April 2022 by Harley Richardson Claire chaired this Academy of Ideas public discussion on 20th April 2022. SPEAKERS: Nick Busvine OBEpartner, Herminius Holdings Ltd; advisory board member and writer, Briefings for Britain; Sevenoaks town councillor; former diplomat, Foreign and Commonwealth Office Professor Bill Durodiéchair of risk and security in international relations, University of Bath Professor Frank Furedisociologist and social commentator; … Read more Categories Events Tags Academy of Ideas Ukraine in the crosshairs of history Tuesday 10 May 2022 Monday 14 March 2022 by Harley Richardson Claire chaired this Academy of Ideas discussion about the conflict in Ukraine, with speakers Frank Furedi, Joan Hoey and Tim Stanley. Categories Events Tags Academy of Ideas Claire discusses the Battle of Ideas on GB News Tuesday 3 January 2023 Wednesday 28 July 2021 by Harley Richardson Claire was a guest on Tonight Live with Dan Wootton on GB News, where she discussed attempts to shut down debate during lockdown, the importance of free and open discussion in a post-pandemic world, and the real-life Battle of Ideas festival event, Open for Debate, taking place in Westminster this Saturday, 31st July. Categories Events , Media Tags John Cronin A year on from Changing Politics for Good Friday 5 March 2021 Saturday 27 February 2021 by Harley Richardson On 29th February 2020, several hundred democrats came together in a Stockport conference centre to discuss how to build on the radical promise of Brexit. Changing Politics For Good: What Next? was organised by Claire Fox and fellow Brexit Party MEP Henrik Overgaard-Nielsen. Claire took part, along with Bruno Kaufmann and Donal Ó Brolcháin, in this discussion organised by the Solonian Democracy Institute. You can hear other discussions from the event here.
